af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. API key via Basic Auth is agent-friendly. Async 202 enrichment requires webhook handling. HubSpot acquisition (2023) creates uncertainty about future. No scope granularity on API key. Coverage gaps in non-NA/EU regions documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key via HTTP Basic Auth (key as username) is functional but not a strong carrier. No scope granularity — key grants full API access. SOC2/GDPR/CCPA compliance. Acquired by HubSpot introduces some supply-chain uncertainty.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: HubSpot acquisition creates future uncertainty — SLA and roadmap unclear post-acquisition. Async enrichment with 202 + webhook is well-documented for error recovery. Agents must handle null/empty enrichment results gracefully.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent developer experience — clean backend API with secret key, no OAuth flow needed for backend agents. OpenAPI reference available. Public vs private metadata distinction requires care. Next.js/React focused but backend API works in any stack.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced everywhere. JWT session tokens with short TTL, separate publishable/secret key architecture. Backend secret key grants full API access (no scopes) — by design but limits granularity. SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA certified. Svix webhook signature verification available.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Managed SaaS with status page and SLA. Active development with stable versioning. Migration guides and deprecation notices provided. Short session token TTL requires proper renewal handling but is well-documented.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Critical gotcha: HTTP 200 does NOT guarantee success — error text can appear in a 200 body; agents must check response body. Multiple auth methods (Basic, URL params, custom headers) create confusion. Separate auth for Cloud Management API vs query HTTP interface. Rich documentation but steep learning curve for columnar semantics.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS on Cloud (ports 8443/9440). Fine-grained GRANT-based privilege system per database/table. Default 'default' user with empty password is a well-known dangerous default — must be locked down. Multiple auth methods increase attack surface. SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA/GDPR on Cloud offering.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: ClickHouse Cloud has SLA and status page; self-hosted is operator's responsibility. Strong versioning with LTS releases. Migration guides available. Retry guidance documented. Eventual deduplication in ReplacingMergeTree means agents must handle count inflation.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. 100 req/min rate limit is very restrictive for agents processing many tasks. Hierarchical workspace > space > folder > list > task model requires significant setup before querying. No idempotency — retrying task creation creates duplicates. Docs lag behind product changes. Custom fields require knowing workspace-specific IDs.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal tokens grant full account access — no scope granularity available. OAuth2 for multi-user apps but personal tokens for agents expose entire workspace. SOC2/GDPR compliance. Token management not well-documented beyond basic header auth.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page exists but SLA not prominently documented. API v2 is current but docs lag product. Mixed breaking changes history. No retry guidance, no idempotency keys — retrying creates duplicates. Restrictive rate limit complicates recovery strategies.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 82). gcloud ADC auth is complex initial setup but standard for GCP ecosystem. Some tools only available in local mode (not remote). DNS rebinding protection disabled by default. SKIP_IAM_CHECK=true by default means permission errors surface late. Excellent for GCP-native workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: GCP enforces HTTPS/TLS on all endpoints. IAM-authenticated with fine-grained GCP roles. Uses gcloud Application Default Credentials — no credentials in code. DNS rebinding protection (ENABLE_HOST_VALIDATION) disabled by default is a concern. GoogleCloudPlatform maintained repo.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud Run has 99.95% SLA and comprehensive status page. MCP server versioned and maintained by GCP team. Error handling in MCP layer is weak (65% quality); errors from GCP propagate with limited context. Underlying Cloud Run is highly reliable.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent documentation with OpenAPI spec and GraphQL support. Granular scoped API tokens are agent-friendly. Zone ID required for most operations — must be fetched first. Rate limit well-documented (1200 req/5min rolling window). FedRAMP authorized for government workloads.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS/HSTS enforced, FedRAMP authorized. Granular API tokens scoped to specific zones, resources, and actions. OAuth2 for third-party apps. Deprecated global API key replaced by scoped tokens. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR/FedRAMP compliance. Industry-leading security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cloudflare has industry-leading uptime (99.99%+ SLA) and real-time global status page. Very stable API with excellent backward compatibility and deprecation notices. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance and idempotency documented. DNS changes propagate near-instantly on Cloudflare network.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. S3-compatible API reduces learning curve for AWS users. HMAC keys work with existing S3 SDKs. Custom domain required for public bucket access. Event notifications require R2 + Queues integration — more complex than S3 events. Zero egress fees is the primary differentiator.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on Cloudflare infrastructure. API tokens with configurable permissions; HMAC keys for S3-compatible access. Scoped permission model available. SOC2/GDPR compliance. Credentials via environment variables; HMAC key separation from account token is good practice.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cloudflare infrastructure SLA and status page. S3-compatible API is inherently stable. Idempotent PUT/DELETE operations. Retry guidance documented. Lack of some S3 features (Object Lock, Inventory) may require workarounds for compliance use cases.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Many endpoints publicly accessible without authentication — very agent-friendly for read-only intelligence gathering. Aggregate data only — easy to misinterpret as per-entity data. Time range parameter inconsistency across endpoints. No published rate limits is a reliability concern for production agents. 15-60 min data lag.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on Cloudflare infrastructure. Bearer token auth with scoped permissions. Many endpoints publicly accessible (appropriate for threat intel sharing). SOC2/GDPR compliance. Cloudflare-maintained Python/TypeScript SDKs. Token optional for public endpoints.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cloudflare status page covers Radar API. Stable versioning. Backward compatible historically. No retry guidance documented; undocumented rate limits create unpredictability. Full GET idempotency. Data has inherent 15-60 min lag that agents must account for.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent docs with OpenAPI spec and GraphQL. Very granular API token permissions. Worker deployments require multipart/form-data — more complex than JSON. V8 isolate environment lacks Node.js APIs — agents deploying Workers must account for this. Account ID and Zone ID both needed depending on operation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, SOC2/ISO27001/PCI-DSS certified. Granular API tokens scoped to accounts, zones, and specific services. OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Workers Secrets for runtime credential storage (never in scripts). Environment variable injection for Workers. Excellent security architecture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cloudflare 99.99%+ SLA with global status page. Stable API versioning with strong backward compatibility. Good deprecation notices. Retry guidance documented; full idempotency on management operations. KV eventual consistency requires agent awareness. Durable Objects offer stronger guarantees.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Seven SDK languages with good coverage. Admin API rate limit (500/hr) is restrictive for bulk operations. HMAC-SHA256 signature with timestamp prevents replay attacks but adds complexity. Eager transformations are asynchronous — agents must poll or use webhooks. Unsigned uploads available for public assets.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 signed requests for Admin API with timestamp validation (prevents replay). API key + secret pattern with no scope granularity — full account access. SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA certified. Unsigned uploads for public assets require careful access design to prevent unauthorized uploads.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page and reasonable SLA. Stable versioning across multi-language SDKs. Deprecation notices provided. Retry guidance documented. Asynchronous eager transformations require webhook/polling patterns for completion detection. Admin API rate limits complicate bulk error recovery.
